# Prism — cognitive-load-aware intent decomposition and logical clarification

> Decomposes complex user intents into structured sub-intents, identifies logical dependencies among them, and generates clarification questions in dependency order to minimize user cognitive load.

## Contents

- [Parameters](#parameters)

## Parameters {#parameters}

| key | type | required | description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ctx.clarify_tokens` | number | optional | Max tokens per clarification phase (default 400) |
| `ctx.decompose_tokens` | number | optional | Max tokens for decomposition phase (default 600) |
| `ctx.max_sub_intents` | number | optional | Maximum sub-intents to extract (default 8) |
| `ctx.task` | string | **required** | Task or request to analyze (required) |

fields.doc: Empty when task was fully specified; otherwise one entry per underspecified sub-intent
fields.name: clarifications

fields.doc: Generated clarification question for this sub-intent
fields.name: question

fields.doc: Sub-intent text carried along for consumer convenience
fields.name: sub_intent

fields.doc: Empty when task was fully specified or no dependencies detected
fields.name: dependencies

fields.doc: Sub-intent index that depends on another (1-based)
fields.name: from

fields.doc: Sub-intent index that must be clarified first (1-based)
fields.name: to

fields.doc: Fully-specified task (equals input task when was_underspecified=false)
fields.name: specified_task

fields.doc: All extracted sub-intents in natural parse order
fields.name: sub_intents

fields.doc: Specification status derived from the bracket tag
fields.name: status

fields.doc: Raw alc.specify response; present only when was_underspecified=true
fields.name: user_response
